CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-26-2023 Continued To Mar-06-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Temporarily Lift Stay To Declare That Lyft Inc.S Arbitration Agreement Controls The Parties Dispute
Off Calendar Sep-13-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Kayla M. Rathjen, Esq., VALLES LAW, P.C, (415) 234-0065 Attorneys for Plaintiff UNLOCK
Daniel T. Rockey, Esq., BRYAN CAVE LEIGHTON PAISNER LLP (415) 675-3400 daniel.rockey@bclplaw.com for Defendant STRIPE, INC.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF UNLOCK's Motion To Temporarily Lift Stay To Declare That Lyft Inc.S Arbitration Agreement Controls The Parties Dispute

Plaintiff's "motion to temporarily lift stay to declare that Lyft, Inc.'s arbitration agreement controls the parties' dispute" is denied.

On March 22, 2023, Stripe, Inc.'s motion to compel plaintiff to arbitration was granted based on multiple arbitration agreements. This court's order stayed the litigation "pending the outcome of arbitration."

Plaintiff now seeks to "temporarily lift stay" based on CCP 1281.4's provision that a court shall "stay the action or proceeding until an arbitration is had in accordance with the order to arbitrate or until such earlier time as the court specifies." Here, the court's order specified the former ("outcome of arbitration") rather than the latter ("such earlier time"). Hence, lifting the stay to have the court intervene in the arbitration would seem to be barred by CCP 1281.4's plain language.

Plaintiff cites two cases on this point. Manatt, Phelps, Rothenberg & Tunney v. Lawrence (1984) 151 Cal.App.3d 1165, 1172-73 regarded the issue of whether a case was "amenable to arbitration," but our plaintiff no longer disputes this. (Mot. 1:5-7.) MKJA, Inc. v. 123 Fit Franchising, LLC (2011) 191 Cal.App.4th 643, 660-61 "assume[s]" that "a trial court possesses some amount of discretion to lift a stay imposed pursuant to section 1281.4," but is unsure of that discretion's scope. What MKJA (and many other cases) is sure of is that "a trial court retains only a very narrow scope of jurisdiction" over a matter compelled to arbitration. (Id. at 661.) Given that admonition, I decline to intervene in a dispute that should be resolved in the arbitration.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

This action was "stayed" by the court's March 22, 2023 order "pending the outcome of arbitration." (Id. at 1:5-6.) No one has attempted to lift the stay. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (302/RBU)
